figures.caption: Figure 1. Qualitative phase diagrams in the (η, α)-plane for fixed depth L (left), together with the same picture in logarithmic coordinates (right), where straight lines correspond to power-law scalings of η and α with respect to L. Different regions correspond to the rigorous regimes obtained from Theorem 1. Specifically, “ODE I” (Corollary 2) yields (4.1) in the ballistic regime αηL = o(1) and η2L = o(1); “ODE II” (Corollary 3) yields (4.2) in the refined deterministic regime αηL = o(1) and η3L = o(1); and “SDE” (Corollary 4) yields the homogenized SDE (3.5) in the diffusive regime αηL = O(1) and η3L = o(1). The region labelled “Static” corresponds to ηL = o(1), so that no non-trivial macroscopic evolution is seen on the time scale tL = ηL, whereas “Failure of approximation” indicates scalings not covered by the present weak-approximation argument. We study the qualitative behavior of the homogenized model in the setting of centered Gaussian weights, for which only the diffusive regime yields non-stationary evolution—see (2.5).
Figure 2. Comparison between the curve γ in Theorem 2 (left) and that of [GLPR25, Theorem 2.13] for d = 128 (right).
Figure 3. Theorem 3 asserts that the second moment approaches the solution of the logistic equation (2.10) when d is large and β is small. Left: n = 200 and β = 0.05. Right: n = 5000 and β = 0.001.
Figure 4. Realizations of solutions to (2.14) in Theorem 4.
